content.problemStatement:
Given a binary search tree, where exactly two nodes of the tree were swapped by mistake. Recover the tree without changing its structure.

Example:




Input Format

The first line of the input contains s single integer NNN — the number of nodes in the binary search tree.
Next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain three space separated characters pi,ci,Rip_i, c_i, R_ipi​,ci​,Ri​, describing the tree with edge informations - pip_ipi​ is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, cic_ici​ is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi​ denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if cic_ici​ is left child of pip_ipi​, else `R`.


Output Format
Complete the given function by recovering the BST without modifying its structure and return the root of the BST.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes in the binary search tree. The next N-1 lines contain three space-separated values: pi, ci, and Ri, where pi is the parent node, ci is the child node, and Ri is either 'L' or 'R', indicating whether ci is the left or right child of pi.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'OK! BST recovered. Well done!' on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The tree described by the 12 nodes has exactly two values swapped, breaking the BST order. The inorder detection finds those two nodes and swaps their values back, restoring the BST. The required message is printed.
A three-node tree where node values are 1,2,3 but the root (value 2) is correct; no swap actually exists, but the algorithm safely does nothing and prints the success message.
A five-node tree with no swapped values. The algorithm detects no issues and prints the success message.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
OK! BST recovered. Well done!
OK! BST recovered. Well done!
OK! BST recovered. Well done!
OK! BST recovered. Well done!
OK! BST recovered. Well done!

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first construct the binary search tree based on the given input. Then, we perform an inorder traversal of the tree to detect the two nodes that are out of order. Once we find these nodes, we swap their values to recover the binary search tree. The time complexity of this approach is O(N), where N is the number of nodes in the tree, since we perform a constant amount of work for each node during the inorder traversal.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) total
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the tree nodes
